title: achillea millefolium var. lanata
intro: Achillea millefolium var. lanata, also known as Woolly Yarrow, is a variety of the perennial plant species Achillea millefolium, characterized by its woolly foliage and white or pink flowers. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, possessing antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and volatile oils. The medicinal properties of Woolly Yarrow contribute to its various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, treating digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Woolly Yarrow can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal remedies to support the immune system and alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and menstrual cramps.
